items.title: Family in battle with council to reinstate brothers' memorial plaques

children.content: A family who lost two brothers to separate freak accidents is fighting to reinstate their beachside memorial plaques after the benches they were on were damaged in a recent storm south of Perth.

items.title: Conspiracy theorist podcaster jailed for stabbing girlfriend to death with dagger

children.content: Tobias Nuttall is sentenced to at least 18 years behind bars for murdering his girlfriend with a 25-centimetre dagger in a "vicious" attack in her suburban Perth home.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man with long brown hair and a moustache, talking while appearing on a video call for a podcast.

items.title: Nedlands MP Jonathan Huston quits WA Liberals amid One Nation hints

children.content: Elected to represent the Liberal heartland seat of Nedlands just last year, Jonathan Huston announces he is quitting the party.

image.altText: A man at an event wearing a Liberal hat.

items.title: Asia has spent 30 years fighting bird flu. Here's what Australia can learn

children.content: Experts say Asia's decades-long experience fighting bird flu has shown there is no magic bullet and that fragmented responses to the disease will fail.

image.altText: A chicken pokes its head out of a red crate while a man in a protective mask and hair net stands behind.

items.title: Women allege sexual assault, underwear theft, drink-spiking at Fortescue mine sites

children.content: Andrew "Twiggy" Forrest's mining giant Fortescue has been slapped with a landmark class action alleging systemic sexual harassment, discrimination and toxic work sites.

image.altText: A graphic image depicting two shadow figures in hard hats with torn red and orange background.

items.title: Tribute to 'respected' WA pilot after fatal flight in plane he built himself

children.content: A friend of 76-year-old pilot Malcolm Vivian remembers him as a "beautiful man" and "special aircraft builder" after his death in a light plane crash near Northam, and says he does not think pilot error was to blame.

articles.title: Man, 76, dies after 'amateur-built replica Spitfire' crashes in WA

children.content: Police say a light plane crashed in Malabaine on Wednesday morning, with the pilot and sole occupant of the aircraft found dead at the scene.

items.title: Coastal town braces for economic pain as mine closure to cost 100 jobs

children.content: Mineral Resources's decision to close the Lucky Bay Garnet Mine will see 110 jobs lost in and around Kalbarri.

image.altText: A picture of a mine sign saying Lucky Bay garnet mine.

items.title: WA man jailed for killing unit manager during argument with sex worker

children.content: A West Australian man has been jailed for fatally striking a 75-year-old man who stepped in to protect his wife and a sex worker during an altercation at a Queensland apartment complex.

items.title: Barring 'ISIS bride' could have triggered High Court challenge

children.content: Legal experts say the Commonwealth would have risked losing a High Court challenge to counterterrorism laws if it stopped the Australian woman from returning home.

items.title: In this remote community, safe drinking water means a daily 600m walk with a wheelbarrow

children.content: Residents of Pandanus Park continue their struggle to access safe drinking water amid a decade-long wait for a solution to dangerous nitrate in their tap supply.

items.title: ASX set to open higher

children.content: The ASX is set to open higher in morning trading after mixed results on Wall Street. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Dezi Freeman case leads to police gun carry changes

children.content: Chief Commissioner Mike Bush says Victoria Police officers in the state's alpine region will soon be permitted to carry long-arm rifles after the Dezi Freeman case.

items.title: A failed forest utopia will watch nervously as the Socceroos play Paraguay

children.content: In rural Paraguay, a small farming town flies Australian flags. When Paraguay plays Australia at the 2026 World Cup today, its residents will be at the edge of their seats.

items.title: Perth's house price surge to continue as Sydney faces $100,000-plus drop

children.content: House price rises of up to 9 per cent in Perth have been forecast for the year ahead, according to Domain, but it's a far different story in Sydney and Melbourne.

items.title: Australia will strengthen its social media ban

children.content: Australia will strengthen its social media ban for young people as a priority amid concerns the powers behind the world-first laws are not strong enough.

items.title: Green back on happy hunting ground with chance to end golf-major drought

children.content: Australian Hannah Green is once again in sparkling form as she returns to the scene of her maiden major win in 2019 with hopes of ending ending her seven-year major drought.

image.altText: Hannah Green stands with her arms folded at Hazletine National Golf Club.

items.title: Can vaccines stop deadly bird flu from spreading?

children.content: With bird flu arriving on our shores, governments, wildlife groups and agricultural organisations are springing into action. But can vaccines limit the economic and environmental damage of the deadly influenza?

articles.title: Sea bird sick with H5 avian flu landed by bodyboarders, too weak to fly to shore

children.content: A surfer who spotted the first bird in South Australia to test positive for H5N1 bird flu says the giant petrel looked fatigued and distressed before it was retrieved by wildlife rescuers.

articles.title: Australia's mammals are at risk of bird flu. This is what we know

items.title: Big battery boom means fewer transmission lines will be needed

children.content: Home batteries hit 600,000 as households play big role in energy transition, but building transmission projects remains "critical", according to the latest AEMO energy plan.

items.title: Tech billionaire accused of assaulting partner, cutting $66k Birkin bag with scissors

children.content: A billionaire tech investor accused of assaulting his former partner and stealing expensive gifts he had bought her has his bail varied so he can visit his mother in Sydney.
